evidence_excerpt: Despite antifungal combination therapy with voriconazole and caspofungin, the patient died 4 weeks after transplantation… The patient died of multiorgan failure on day 28.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Persistent bilateral pleural effusions associated with pneumothorax and slight pneumomediastinum (day 23), positive blood culture with septate hyphae (day 27), culminating in multiorgan failure (day 28).

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: On day 23, TDM showed persistence of bilateral pleural effusions associated with pneumothorax and slight pneumomediastinum; bronchial fibroscopy highlighted pseudomembranes.
Surgery was decided on day 25 to suture the left pulmonary parenchyma and to remove pleural clots.
A blood culture was positive on day 27; direct smear showed septate hyphae, indicating fungemia.
The patient died of multiorgan failure on day 28.

notes_on_uncertainty: Index time is taken as the day disseminated invasive fungal infection was proven from pleural fluid (day 19) and antifungal therapy was initiated. Quality-of-life assessments are inferred from severe complications and terminal outcome; detailed functional activity descriptions were not available.
